checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the GAS_CAP constant exactly 16777216 (2^24) in all code paths?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate all definitions of GAS_CAP or MAX_GAS_LIMIT constants in the codebase. 2. Verify each constant equals exactly 16777216 (0x1000000 or 2^24). 3. Search for hardcoded values that should reference the constant. 4. Submit test transactions with gasLimit at boundary values (16777215, 16777216, 16777217) to verify consistent acceptance/rejection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-1, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, tb-005. Network split risk if clients use different cap values.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does txpool reject transactions with gasLimit > 16777216?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16777217 via RPC. 2. Verify the transaction is rejected with MAX_GAS_LIMIT_EXCEEDED error. 3. Query the mempool to confirm the transaction was not added. 4. Repeat with gasLimit values 16777216 (should accept) and higher values (should reject).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-2, Trust Boundary: tb-001. First line of defense at transaction entry point.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does P2P-relayed transaction validation match local validation?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Configure a test peer to relay a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216. 2. Verify the receiving node rejects it identically to local submission. 3. Check that no code path bypasses validation based on transaction source. 4. Review code to ensure P2P and local paths converge on same validation logic.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-3, Trust Boundary: tb-002. Malicious peers could attempt to poison mempools.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation reject blocks containing over-cap transactions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct a block containing a transaction with gasLimit = 16777217. 2. Propagate the block via P2P to a test node. 3. Verify the entire block is rejected as invalid. 4. Check that the rejection is logged with appropriate error details. 5. Verify no partial state changes occurred.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-4, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Last line of defense for consensus.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation check ALL transactions without skipping?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct blocks with violating transactions at different positions (first, middle, last, only). 2. Verify each block is rejected regardless of violating transaction position. 3. Review validation loop code for early exits, sampling, or short-circuits. 4. Test with maximum transaction count blocks.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-5, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-005. Position-dependent validation bypass risk.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the entire block rejected when any transaction violates gas cap?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct a block with 99 valid transactions and 1 over-cap transaction. 2. Propagate to test node and verify entire block is rejected. 3. Verify no transactions from the block were executed or persisted. 4. Check that partial acceptance mode does not exist in the codebase.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-6, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Partial acceptance causes consensus divergence.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is per-transaction gas cap independent of block gas limit?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Configure a validator with block gas limit > 16777216. 2. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16777217. 3. Verify the transaction is rejected despite block limit being higher. 4. Review code to ensure per-tx cap is not derived from block limit. 5. Test both txpool and block validation paths.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-7, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-004, tb-005. Cap confusion could bypass protection.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are both txpool and block validation independently enforcing the gas cap?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all gas cap check locations in code. 2. Verify at least one check exists in txpool validation. 3. Verify at least one check exists in block validation. 4. Test bypassing txpool (e.g., via Engine API block construction) and verify block validation still catches it. 5. Ensure neither layer assumes the other has validated.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-8, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, tb-005. Single point of failure risk.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the gas cap value 16777216 identical at both enforcement points?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the gas cap constant definition(s) in the codebase. 2. Verify txpool references the same constant as block validation. 3. Check for any duplicated or hardcoded cap values that could diverge. 4. Static analysis: ensure both paths use the same constant or identical value. 5. Test transaction with gasLimit in the theoretical discrepancy range if separate constants exist.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-9, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003. Inconsistent caps cause consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Is a rejected transaction dropped and never forwarded to block building?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216 to txpool. 2. Verify the rejection is final and immediate. 3. Monitor internal queues to ensure the transaction never reaches block builder. 4. Review code for any retry or fallback paths that could resurrect rejected transactions. 5. Test edge cases like concurrent submission attempts.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-10, Internal edge: tb-004. Defense-in-depth verification.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the comparison operator <= (not <) used for gas cap check?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review code for all gas cap comparison operators. 2. Verify condition is gasLimit <= 16777216 (accept) or gasLimit > 16777216 (reject). 3. Submit transactions with gasLimit = 16777215 (accept), 16777216 (accept), 16777217 (reject). 4. Cross-client test to ensure all clients use the same comparison semantics.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-11, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Off-by-one causes DoS or consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do all clients use identical comparison semantics (> not >=)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 16777216 to each major Ethereum client. 2. Verify all clients accept the transaction identically. 3. Submit gasLimit = 16777217 and verify all clients reject. 4. Document the exact comparison operator used in each client's source code. 5. Alert if any divergence is found.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-12, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003. Single transaction can split the network.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is RLP encoding of gasLimit strictly validated across all clients?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft transactions with non-canonical RLP encoding of gasLimit (leading zeros, oversized integers). 2. Submit to each client and verify uniform rejection. 3. Craft a block with non-canonical gasLimit encoding near the 16777216 boundary. 4. Verify all clients parse identically or uniformly reject. 5. Document any parsing differences.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-13, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Encoding ambiguity causes consensus divergence.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is per-tx cap enforced even when block gas limit exceeds it?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create a block with header gas limit > 16777216 containing an over-cap transaction. 2. Propagate to test nodes and verify rejection. 3. Review code to confirm per-tx cap is checked independently of block.gasLimit. 4. Test with validator configured to set high block gas limits.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-14, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Per-tx cap must not be confused with block limit.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas cap validation O(1) per transaction with minimal memory?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review validation code to confirm it's a simple integer comparison. 2. Benchmark block validation with maximum transaction count. 3. Verify no per-transaction memory allocations occur during gas cap check. 4. Measure CPU time and memory for worst-case blocks. 5. Verify validation time is proportional to transaction count.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-15, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Inefficient validation enables timing attacks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation catch over-cap transactions from direct block construction?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct a block directly via Engine API (bypassing txpool). 2. Include a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216. 3. Verify block validation rejects the block. 4. Review code to ensure block validation does not trust that txpool already validated. 5. Test with MEV block builders in the validation chain.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-16, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Block builders could bypass txpool.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does transaction replacement re-validate gasLimit?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit a valid transaction with gasLimit = 16777216. 2. Submit a replacement (same nonce, higher fee) with gasLimit = 16777217. 3. Verify the replacement is rejected. 4. Review transaction replacement code to ensure all fields are revalidated. 5. Test various replacement scenarios (EIP-1559 fee bumps, etc.).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-17, Trust Boundary: tb-001. TOCTOU in replacement could bypass validation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gasLimit covered by the transaction signature?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create a signed transaction with gasLimit = 16777216. 2. Modify the gasLimit field after signing (set to 16777217). 3. Submit to a node and verify signature verification fails. 4. Review transaction signing/verification code to confirm gasLimit is in the signed payload. 5. Test for all transaction types (legacy, EIP-1559, EIP-4844).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-18, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002. P2P tampering must be detectable.
